everything.dev is an open runtime for apps on NEAR.

The site is published from every.near/everything.dev.
It is composed at runtime from public configuration rather than built as one fixed application bundle.

Core architecture:
- host: runtime shell, auth boundary, request handling, runtime config loader
- auth: authentication plugin loaded through every-plugin (Better-Auth, NEAR SIWN, organizations, API keys)
- ui: remote interface loaded through Module Federation
- api: remote backend loaded through every-plugin

Canonical runtime model:
- bos.config.json is the source of truth for runtime composition
- config can inherit from other published records through bos:// references
- metadata can be layered on top, but does not replace the runtime manifest

Why it matters:
- sites can share the same host while varying UI and API composition
- runtime can change through published config instead of rebuild-only deployment
- software remains more portable, inspectable, and extensible over time

Useful public paths:
- /
- /about
- /apps
- /README.md
- /skill.md
- /manifest.json
